seoTitle: How to Create a Test Case in Jira Zephyr Scale
metaDescription: Learn how to build a test case in Zephyr Scale within Jira, covering steps, expected results, and linking it back to related issues.
intro: Zephyr Scale adds structured test management on top of Jira, letting QA teams create and track test cases alongside the issues they relate to. This guide covers building a test case from scratch.

steps.heading: Open Zephyr Scale in your Jira project
steps.body: Navigate to the Zephyr Scale section within your Jira project to access test case management.

steps.heading: Create a new test case
steps.body: Start a new test case and give it a clear name describing what it verifies.

steps.heading: Define test steps
steps.body: Add the individual steps required to execute the test, describing the actions to take at each stage.

steps.heading: Set expected results
steps.body: For each step, specify the expected outcome so testers can clearly identify a pass or fail result.

steps.heading: Link the test case to related issues
steps.body: Connect the test case to the story or requirement it verifies, keeping traceability between development and testing.

steps.heading: Organize the test case into a folder or cycle
steps.body: Place the test case into the appropriate folder or test cycle so it's easy to find and execute during a testing pass.

faq.question: Do I need a separate license to use Zephyr Scale?
faq.answer: Yes, Zephyr Scale is a Jira app that typically requires its own license or subscription beyond your base Jira plan.

faq.question: Can a test case be linked to multiple issues?
faq.answer: Yes, a single test case can be linked to more than one related issue if it verifies functionality tied to multiple stories or requirements.

faq.question: How do I run a test case once it's created?
faq.answer: You add the test case to a test cycle, then execute it step by step, recording pass or fail results as you go.

faq.question: Can I reuse test cases across different test cycles?
faq.answer: Yes, test cases in Zephyr Scale are typically designed to be reusable across multiple test cycles rather than created fresh each time.
